title: /1 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: A /1 subnet mask splits the entire IPv4 address space in half. It is rarely used in local networking but essential for global routing tables.

title: /8 CIDR (Class A) to Subnet Mask
seo_content: The /8 prefix is the classic Class A network, used by large organizations and ISPs for massive internal address spacing.

title: /16 CIDR (Class B) to Subnet Mask
seo_content: A /16 subnet mask provides 65,534 usable addresses, common in corporate headquarters and data center environments.

title: /20 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: The /20 mask is ideal for large campuses, offering over 4,000 usable host addresses.

title: /21 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: A /21 subnet provides 2,046 usable IP addresses, perfect for large-scale enterprise departments.

title: /22 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: The /22 prefix allows for 1,022 hosts, commonly used to aggregate multiple /24 networks.

title: /23 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: A /23 subnet mask provides exactly 510 usable IP addresses, doubling the capacity of a standard /24.

title: /24 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: A /24 subnet mask is the standard for small business networks, offering 254 usable IP addresses.

title: /30 CIDR to Subnet Mask Converter
seo_content: Used for point-to-point router links, a /30 mask minimizes IP waste.

title: Bits to Bytes Converter

data.extra: 8 bits = 1 Byte. Bits are for speed, Bytes for size.

seo_content: Convert bits to bytes instantly. 8 bits always equal 1 byte in standard computing.

title: Megabytes (MB) to Gigabytes (GB) Converter

data.extra: 1024 Megabytes = 1 Gigabyte in binary (RAM), 1000 MB = 1 GB in decimal (Disk).

seo_content: Easily convert MB to GB using the 1024 binary standard.
